title: Venezuelans call for help to rescue survivors from rubble after twin earthquakes
subhead: International aid has only just begun to arrive, while local residents have been trying to clear the rubble with their bare hands since Wednesday. As the hours go by, the death toll of 920, along with 50,000 missing, is likely to rise.

title: In Ukraine, those who leave and those who stay: Life on hold in Sloviansk and Kramatorsk
Lebanon and Israel sign framework agreement in Washington for 'lasting peace and security'
France heatwave: Why adaptation and mitigation must go hand in hand
subhead: To respond to climate change, authorities must urgently implement policies to make rising temperatures bearable for the public, without abandoning addressing the root cause of climate change.

title: From a Russian prison to a European music festival, the escape of a young anti-Putin singer
subhead: On June 12, artist Diana Loginova performed among the stars of Russia's dissident music scene at the Outloud festival in Warsaw. She took to the stage after managing to escape from Russia. In 2025, she was in prison for singing songs condemned by the Kremlin.

title: Dembélé shines at World Cup, silencing critics
subhead: After scoring a hat trick and named man of the match as France defeated Norway 4-1 on Friday, Ousmane Dembélé is making an impact at the 2026 World Cup. The long-awaited breakthrough with the French national team has finally arrived.

title: 'They have the same criminal mentality': One year after replacing Wagner, Russia's Africa Corps steps up abuses in Mali
subhead: 'Mali: One year of Africa Corps' (2/2). Although directly overseen by the Russian Ministry of Defense, Africa Corps soldiers have already been implicated in numerous executions, sometimes accompanied by gruesome staging.

250 years of American independence: Thomas Jefferson and John Adams, 'the North and South poles of the American Revolution'
subhead: 'America 250' (4/13). Fifty years to the day after the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, two of its architects died: the enigmatic Thomas Jefferson and the impulsive John Adams. This coincidence only strengthened, among the population, the sense of an unprecedented destiny promised to their nation.

title: Ukraine's Donbas rear area, from Sloviansk to Kramatorsk, is under constant Russian pressure
subhead: 'Donbas: The War of Attrition' (1/2). Just a few kilometers from one of Ukraine's fiercest fronts, these two cities are under drone strikes that make residents' lives very difficult. Both civilians and soldiers fear the bombings will intensify.
byline: Thomas d’Istria and  Virginie NGuyen Hoang

title: 'Impossible to sleep': A night in sweltering France, in search of a cool spot
subhead: With a heatwave bearing down, the French have taken to living after dark, chasing relief from the sun's lingering grip. From Paris to Saint-Malo, by way of Orléans, parks and pools stirred to life as soon as the day ended.
